title: Crypsinus lamprophyllus
intro: Crypsinus lamprophyllus, also known as Cryptolepis sanguinolenta, is a plant species native to West Africa, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including treating malaria, fever, and various gastrointestinal issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Crypsinus lamprophyllus include alkaloids, such as cryptolepine and sanguinarine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Crypsinus lamprophyllus include decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
